body:
**Context**

One-shot bootstrap that has already run. It also writes an out-of-date `.gitignore` heredoc that, if re-run, would clobber the real `.gitignore` (missing `.vite/`, `coverage/`, `package-lock.json`).

**TODO**

`git rm scripts/init_repo.sh`. Update `docs/CONTRIBUTING.md` if it references the script.

**Acceptance**

File is gone; no dangling references.
title: [hygiene] Delete `scripts/init_repo.sh`

body:
**Context**

`data/fa_data.json` holds the 10-sample `blue_export.txt`-derived dataset. The current demo is 2 samples (`V059_4-5` + `gRNA3_1-1`) from `data/seed_v059-4-5_grna3-1-1.json` / `public/demo/*.fsa`. The `fa-data-schema` CI job asserts exactly 10 samples, which locks in the staleness. `fa_data.json` is still referenced by `scripts/build_artifact.py:25`, `scripts/ingest_to_kb.py:27`, and `skills/clc-visualizations/SKILL.md:74` — so it's not fully orphaned, but it is stale relative to the live viewer.

**TODO**

Pick one of:

- Regenerate `fa_data.json` from the current FSA demo pair and update the CI assertion to match.
- Delete `fa_data.json` and its CI job if nothing downstream reads it; `scripts/build_artifact.py` can regenerate on demand.

**Acceptance**

`data/fa_data.json` either matches what the viewer actually ships, or is gone.
title: [data] `data/fa_data.json` is stale (10-sample legacy dataset)

body:
**Context** 

Several public-facing files reference Greg's local paths and lab-internal infra that external readers cannot have:
- `skills/fragment-viewer/SKILL.md:30` — `/mnt/d/University of Michigan Dropbox/Gregory Farnum/...`
- `skills/clc-construct-registry/SKILL.md:22` — same
- `docs/GRNA_CATALOG.md:66`, `src/FragmentViewer.jsx:1552` — `/mnt/d/Reference_Files/...GRCh38...fasta`
- README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, CONTRIBUTING, LAB_INTEGRATIONS, TUTORIAL all refer to `~/lab_knowledge.db`, `~/.claude/hooks/`, `~/repos/ont-ecosystem/`, `lab-papers`, `lab-wiki`, `lab-query-router`, `fragment-viewer-ingest.timer`

**Confirmation**

`skills/fragment-viewer/SKILL.md:30` has `/mnt/d/University of Michigan Dropbox/Gregory Farnum/...`; `skills/clc-construct-registry/SKILL.md:22` has the same path; `docs/LAB_INTEGRATIONS.md:115` has `/home/farnum248/repos/...` (username leak); `docs/GRNA_CATALOG.md:66` has `/mnt/d/Reference_Files/...`; `src/FragmentViewer.jsx:1553` has same in a comment. Also `.project/workspace.yaml:10` has the Dropbox path.

**TODO**

- For the SKILL.md "canonical repo location" fields: make the GitHub URL canonical; note the lab mirror as optional.
- For the `farnum248` username leak: rewrite the example path as `$LAB_PAPERS_REPO` env var or delete the example.
- For ecosystem-wide infra references: add an "Athey lab integration (internal)" section at the end of each doc and move them there, or migrate to `.project/` which is already the internal-notes home.
- Remove any internal information for public-facing distribution.

**Acceptance**

`git grep -E "farnum248|mnt/d/University|mnt/d/Reference_Files"` returns zero hits outside comments in code and `.project/`. Public-facing top-level docs don't assume the reader has a `~/lab_knowledge.db`.
title: [critical] [docs] [privacy] Strip lab-internal paths + identifiers from public docs

body:
**Context**

Line 5: `const FSA = "/mnt/d/Downloads/30-1313048433/30-1313048433/gRNA3_1-1.fsa"`. Lines 8-10 skip via `fs.existsSync`. `public/demo/gRNA3_1-1.fsa` exists and can replace it. The test `fs.existsSync(FSA)` check always fails in CI and for every non-Greg developer, so the three assertions silently skip. The repo already ships equivalent fixtures at `public/demo/V059_4-5.fsa` and `public/demo/gRNA3_1-1.fsa`.

**TODO**

- Replace the hardcoded path with `new URL("../public/demo/gRNA3_1-1.fsa", import.meta.url)` or a path.resolve off `process.cwd()`.
- Remove the `if (!fs.existsSync(...)) it.skip(...)` branch; the fixture is always present now.
- Optional: add a parallel test case against `V059_4-5.fsa` so both dyes-per-sample orderings are covered.

**Acceptance**

CI actually runs the three assertions in the `ABIF JS parser on real .fsa` block.
title: [bug] [test] `tests/fsa_parser.test.mjs` hardcodes a WSL-local path · `P0` `test`

body:
**Context**

Line 2950: `useMemo(() => identifyPeaks(DATA.peaks, cfg), [cfg])` — `dataKey` is NOT in the deps array. Compare with `totalPeaks` on the very next line, which correctly depends on `[dataKey]`. When drag-drop mutates `DATA.peaks` and bumps `dataKey` without changing `cfg`, the memo will serve stale results. The `<div key={dataKey}>` remount is at line 3046, below the memo, so the parent memo is never torn down.

**TODO**

- Write a vitest / RTL test that mounts `FragmentViewer`, simulates `handleNewPeaks(differentPeaks)`, and asserts downstream identity reflects `differentPeaks`.

**Acceptance**

Either a passing new test demonstrating no-stale-memo under ingest, or a fix + that same test.
title: [bug] Stale `results` memo on drag-drop ingest

body:
**Context**

Five locations hold per-dye hex values, and they disagree:
- `DyeChip` (line 69): `G=#16a34a Y=#ca8a04 R=#dc2626`
- `DYE` const (line 1098): `G=#2e9e4a Y=#b8860b R=#d32f2f`
- `DYE_PALETTES.default` (line 1113): same as `DYE`
- `DYE_HEX` (line 2244): same as `DyeChip`
- `tailwind.config.js::theme.extend.colors.dye`: same as `DyeChip`

**TODO**

- Pick one set. `#16a34a / #ca8a04 / #dc2626` (the Tailwind 600 family) matches the already-committed theme tokens; use those.
- Make `DYE_PALETTES.default` the single runtime source of truth for rendering code.
- Delete `DYE_HEX`. Have `DyeChip` call `resolveDyeColor(dye, palette)` instead of its inline map.
- `DYE.color` is referenced in a few places for convenience; point it at `DYE_PALETTES.default[d]` or remove the `.color` field and update call sites.
- Add a vitest case that asserts `DYE_PALETTES.default.G === "#16a34a"` (or whichever is picked) so future drift trips CI.

**Acceptance**

One grep for `#1e6fdb` in `src/` returns one definition. Colors on screen are unchanged for users on the default palette.
title: [bug] [ui] Collapse four drifted dye-color palettes into one source

body:
**Context**

`src/FragmentViewer.jsx:1383` has `export function classifyPeaks(sampleData, constructSeq, targetStart, targetEnd, constructSize, componentSizes, assemblyProducts, grnaCatalog, dyeOffsets, heightThreshold, matchTol, clusterTol, overhangsToConsider)`. The body calls `matchLabCatalog(g)`, which reads module-level `LAB_GRNA_CATALOG`; the `grnaCatalog` parameter is never referenced.

**TODO**

- Change `matchLabCatalog` to take an optional `catalog` parameter (default `LAB_GRNA_CATALOG`), mirroring `inventoryStatus` which already does this.
- Thread the caller's `grnaCatalog` through `classifyPeaks` → `matchLabCatalog`.
- Add a vitest case that calls `classifyPeaks` with a custom catalog and asserts the catalog-matched identity reflects the custom entry, not the module default.

**Acceptance** 

The `grnaCatalog` arg is either wired through or removed from the signature. No behavioral change on the default path.
title: [bug] `classifyPeaks` declares an unused `grnaCatalog` parameter
